g.text: Changes to be made based on what UI/UX will be like
How will username/userIdentifier be known, will it be given on ingress to this flow?
User would like to submit a transaction
User clicks button to start transaction process
GET user passkey data to create passkey transaction payload (userIdentifier (or whatever username))
Retrieve credential record data, (compressed) passkey public key, account public key, passkey signature from db using username/(userIdentifier (or whatever username)
Send user's data to "Wallet Backend" (credential record id, public keys, signature)
Send payload with user passkey data to client
Account Signature of Compressed Passkey Public Key
Credential IDs associated with this user
User fills out form (or whatever it is) to submit a transaction
On submit, actual transaction call to Frequency is created
Assuming this transaction call will be done client side and sent to blockchain
Create passkey call data to submit to Frequency using transaction call, account signature, and account public key
accountOwnershipProof: { Account Signature of Passkey Public Key },
call: PasskeyCall (the one created in the step above)
challenge: Passkey Call Data (in UInt8 array format),
timeout: Number in seconds (60000 is fine),
allowCredentials: Array of authenticators credentialIds, this will need to be gotten from db,
Signs and returns credential data if successful, error if not
- authenticatorData: Similar to authData in registration but doesn’t include the public key
- origin: Server must validate that the origin string matches with application origin
- type: Server validates that this is a “WebAuthn.get” object
- Signature: This will effectively be the passkey call data signature since that was the challenge given
Parse signature, authenticatorData, clientExtensionJSON and ClientDataJSON from credential data
passkeySignature: Array.from(base64UrlToUint8Array(Passkey Signature from Credential Data)),
passkeyCall: Passkey Call Data (like the challenge)),
Create transaction from Passkey Payload and send to blockchain as unsigned extrinsic
Get some verification that transaction was successful
